what_it_does: Acts as the package marker and design manifest for mcp_face/tools; its docstring states that each resource module exposes register(mcp, suite), defines one consolidated parameterized tool, and is auto-discovered by server.py via pkgutil without editing server.py.
observations: The file contains only a module docstring and no executable code or symbols.
It documents a file-discovered registration pattern: adding a resource means adding a file.
Each tool module is expected to expose a single register(mcp, suite) function.
Tools are consolidated and parameterized by an op/by/kind selector, not a flat list of per-operation tools.
It references an external standing law document: build-prep/cognition-self-improvement/MCP-DESIGN-PRINCIPLE.md.
It compares the discovery pattern to other registries named roles, projections, and nodes.
standouts: A package __init__.py used primarily as an architectural contract rather than for imports.
The file-discovered plugin convention where adding a file adds a tool.
The one-consolidated-tool-per-resource design with an op/by/kind selector.
conventions: Follows the Python package marker convention via __init__.py.
Uses the docstring as a self-documenting module manifest.
Does not define __all__, imports, or runtime enforcement of the documented contract.
concerns: The contract is only documented; there is no file-local validation that modules follow it.
No __all__ or explicit package exports are declared.
It references an external design-principle document that is not present in this file.
notes: This file is the mcp_face/tools package header: it records the design law for how MCP tool modules are discovered and structured, but contains no registration logic itself.
questions: What is the exact schema for the op/by/kind selector?
What object or interface does suite represent?
How does server.py perform pkgutil discovery and call register?
What are the roles, projections, and nodes registries and how closely do they mirror this pattern?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to serve as a package-level design manifest, and that is exactly what the file does, though it contains no implementation beyond the docstring.
intention_signals: The idea is to make adding an MCP tool as simple as dropping a resource file into mcp_face/tools, with server.py automatically picking it up, while avoiding a proliferation of flat per-operation tools by centralizing each resource behind a single selector-driven tool.
novelty: Using __init__.py as a standing-law manifest for a file-discovered MCP tool registry, and mandating one consolidated parameterized tool per resource instead of many flat operation-specific tools.
contribution: It contributes the package boundary and the documented contract that each resource module exposes register(mcp, suite) and defines one consolidated parameterized tool, enabling a file-discovered, server-agnostic registration pattern.
summary_for_embedding: Package marker and design manifest for mcp_face/tools declaring that each resource module exposes register(mcp, suite) as a single consolidated parameterized tool and is auto-discovered by server.py via pkgutil.
intention_for_embedding: To enable file-discovered, resource-oriented MCP tool registration without modifying server.py.
